title: Bitter Crumbly Light Egyptian Dates Crumble
description: A vegan dessert featuring light and bitter Egyptian dates topped with a crumbly crumble made from chia seeds, mixed with chimichurri sauce for added flavor.

ingredients.name: 1 cup pitted light Egyptian dates
ingredients.notes: Use dried or fresh, depending on availability.

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons chia seeds
ingredients.notes: Soak in a bit of water before mixing with other ingredients.

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup nutritional yeast (optional for extra umami)
ingredients.notes: Adjust to taste, can be omitted.

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons chimichurri sauce (store-bought or homemade with parsley, garlic, vinegar)
ingredients.notes: Add extra flavor to the crumble.

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup unsweetened almond milk (or any non-dairy milk for richness and creaminess)
ingredients.notes: Used as a binding agent in the crumble.

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons cornstarch
ingredients.notes: For thickening the sauce when making chimichurri.

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup water (or vegan milk for richer texture)
ingredients.notes: Used to mix with cornstarch and create a glaze.

instructions: Soak chia seeds in almond milk overnight or for at least 30 minutes.
In a blender, puree dates until smooth. Add lemon juice and blend again for extra zestiness.
Combine the date mixture with soaked chia seeds and nutritional yeast (if using). Mix well.
For chimichurri sauce: Mix together all chimichurri ingredients in a bowl until well combined.
Add chimichurri to the date and chia mixture, stirring continuously for about 2 minutes. Let it rest for a few minutes.
In another mixing bowl, whisk together cornstarch with water (or vegan milk). Slowly pour over the date-chia mixture while blending.
Pour into a shallow dish or mold. Chill in fridge for at least an hour before serving to set and crispiness is maintained.
Serve with fresh greens as a salad dressing or drizzle liberally on top of crumble to add extra layers of flavors.
